_verification_note: Drafted by structural derivation from BS 7671:2018+A2 (KS 1700:2018 adopts BS 7671 substantially per Annex E). Engineering values reproduced verbatim. KS 1700 has no §722 (EV charging); engineers must reference IEC 60364-7-722:2018 directly for EV installations. Final verification against the published KS 1700:2018 PDF required.
_ks_adoption_pathway: KS 1700:2018 Annex E — adopts BS 7671:2018+A2 Part 7 with exception: no §722 EV charging
_title: KS 1700:2018 Part 7 — Special installations or locations (bathrooms, pools, agricultural, EV via IEC routing)

_ks_deviation_from_bs.summary: KS 1700:2018 has no §722 (EV charging). KS Annex E §VIII directs to IEC 60364-7-722:2018 for EV installations.
_ks_deviation_from_bs.rationale: KS 1700:2018 first edition predates BS 7671 AMD 2 (2022); EV charging is in scope for the next KS revision (estimated 2024 cycle)
_ks_deviation_from_bs.engineering_impact: EV installations in Kenya apply IEC 60364-7-722 directly; future KS revision will incorporate native §722. Until then, KS 1700 §411 baseline + IEC 60364-7-722 specifics combine for EV designs.
_ks_deviation_from_bs.future_status: Expected native §722 clause in next KS revision

_note: Detailed requirements for specific locations where normal protective measures are insufficient or modified. RCD-only requirements are covered in reg411-rcd-requirements.json — this file covers the FULL Part 7 requirements per section.

section_701_bathrooms_and_shower_rooms.scope: Rooms or other locations containing a fixed bath or shower. Includes wet rooms, en-suite bathrooms, accessible WCs with shower.

zone_0.definition: Inside the bath/shower basin — the volume within the basin itself
zone_0.allowed_equipment: Only equipment specifically designed for the location and operating at SELV ≤ 12V. Underwater lights for spas/baths.

zone_1.definition: Above zone 0, extending to 2.25m above floor (or higher of shower head). Excludes the volume below the bath if accessible only by tool.
zone_1.ip_rating: IPx4 minimum (IPx5 where water jets used per Reg 701.512.2)
zone_1.allowed_equipment: Fixed water heaters, fixed luminaires, shower pumps, whirlpool units — ALL must be hardwired and SELV-supplied where possible. No socket outlets except SELV ≤ 12V.

zone_2.definition: 0.6m horizontally beyond zone 1 boundary, to 2.25m height
zone_2.allowed_equipment: Fixed luminaires, hand dryers, towel rails. Shaver sockets ONLY (must comply with BS EN 61558-2-5). Other socket outlets only if ≥ 3m from any zone boundary (in larger rooms).

outside_zones.definition: Anywhere in the room beyond the zone boundaries
outside_zones.ip_rating: IPx0 (i.e. no special IP) unless splash exposure expected
outside_zones._socket_outlet_rule: Socket outlets permitted but only ≥ 3m from boundary of zone 1 (Reg 701.512.3)

section_701_bathrooms_and_shower_rooms.rcd_requirement: 30mA RCD on ALL circuits supplying the room — including outside zones, including circulating lighting, including fixed equipment (Reg 701.411.3.3).
section_701_bathrooms_and_shower_rooms.supplementary_equipotential_bonding: Required (Reg 701.415.2) between all simultaneously accessible exposed-conductive-parts and extraneous-conductive-parts in zones 1 and 2, UNLESS all of these conditions met: (a) main equipotential bonding present and verified, (b) all circuits to room have 30mA RCD, (c) all extraneous-conductive-parts bonded to MET. In practice, supplementary bonding is usually omitted in modern installations meeting these conditions.
common_pitfalls: Socket outlet too close to shower screen — fails zone 2 horizontal distance
IP44 fitting in zone 0 — needs IPx7
Missing 30mA RCD on bathroom lighting — non-compliant since AMD 2 for domestic anyway
Decorative downlight installed by trade not aware of zone requirements
Fan ducting that creates electrical path between zones — bonding overlooked

section_702_swimming_pools.scope: Pools, paddling pools, basins of swimming pools, fountains.

zones.zone_0: Interior of pool basin — water itself + tiled basin walls
zones.zone_2: Beyond zone 1 to 1.5m horizontal, 2.5m vertical (commercial only — domestic pools have no zone 2)

ip_ratings.zone_1: IPx5 (private), IPx4 (public) where water jets cleaning may occur

section_702_swimming_pools.rcd_requirement: 30mA RCD on all circuits supplying or passing through zones 0, 1, 2 (Reg 702.415.1)
section_702_swimming_pools.equipotential_bonding: All metallic parts (ladders, drains, handrails, reinforcement) must be bonded to local equipotential bonding bar, which is connected to MET (Reg 702.415.2)
section_702_swimming_pools.underwater_luminaires: 12V SELV ONLY. Transformer must be at least 3.5m from pool edge (Reg 702.55.4)

section_703_saunas.scope: Rooms containing sauna heaters where steam/dry heat is used.

zones.zone_1: Around the heater — high temperature zone
zones.zone_2: Within the sauna room, away from heater
zones.zone_3: Higher level — above 1.5m height for accessories

key_requirements: Cables must be rated for sauna temperatures (typically 170°C in zone 1) — silicone insulated or equivalent
Light fittings: IPx4 minimum, T-rated for ambient up to 125°C
30mA RCD on all sauna circuits except the heater (Reg 703.411.3.3)
All control gear OUTSIDE the sauna
Only the sauna heater supply may pass through the sauna walls

section_704_construction_and_demolition_.scope: Temporary electrical installations on construction or demolition sites.
key_requirements: Reduced Low Voltage (RLV) 110V (55V to earth) for portable tools — 'yellow' supplies via site transformer (Reg 704.410.3)
30mA RCD on ALL socket outlets, regardless of rating (Reg 704.410.3.10)
500mA S-type RCD at site origin
TT or TN-S earthing — TN-C-S (PME) requires special precautions due to risk of broken PEN at site cable damage
All flexible cables to be H07RN-F or equivalent (heavy duty rubber)
Site distribution boards to BS 4363 (transportable assemblies for construction sites)
Inspection and testing every 3 months (Reg 704.633)

section_705_agricultural_and_horticultur.scope: Farms, stables, animal housing, glasshouses, horticultural enterprises.
key_requirements: All cables must have mechanical protection (SWA or in conduit) — vehicle and animal damage risk
30mA RCD on socket outlets ≤ 32A (Reg 705.411.1)
100mA delayed RCD on final circuits supplying equipment in animal areas (Reg 705.411.1)
300mA RCD at the supply origin to detect fire risk from earth leakage in fodder/straw areas
Equipotential bonding between all metallic parts accessible to animals (Reg 705.415)
Locations where livestock may contact equipment — touch voltage limit 25V (vs 50V for human-only areas)
Lighting in animal housing should be NOT energised in animal areas during animal access (avoid spooking, equipment damage)

section_708_caravan_parks_and_camping_pa.scope: Distribution boards and socket outlets at touring caravan/camping pitches.
key_requirements: TT earthing per pitch — independent earth electrode for each pitch socket assembly (Reg 708.411.4)
Maximum one caravan per socket outlet (Reg 708.553)
30mA RCD per socket outlet (Reg 708.553.1.7)
Socket outlet to BS EN 60309-2 (commando type), 16A blue, IP44 minimum
Maximum 25m flexible cable between pitch socket and caravan inlet (BS 7671:2018 + practice)
Visual warning notice required at each pitch socket about safe use

section_709_marinas.scope: Berths in marinas for boats, sleeping accommodation on board not connected to shore.
key_requirements: TT per pontoon — independent earth electrode for each pontoon distribution assembly (Reg 709.411.4)
30mA RCD per socket outlet (no shared RCDs)
Maximum 4 socket outlets per distribution assembly
Socket outlets to BS EN 60309-2, IP67 (boats use blue 16A or 32A)
Galvanic isolation: where shore PE and boat PE are connected, install isolation transformer or galvanic isolator to prevent stray currents corroding hull
Inspection annually (Reg 709.633)

section_710_medical_locations.scope: Locations where electrical equipment is used on patients in medical treatment. Three groups.

group_0.definition: Medical locations where no medical electrical equipment in contact with the patient is used (e.g. consulting rooms, general clinical offices)
group_0.supply: Standard TN-S or TN-C-S, RCD per general requirements

group_1.definition: Medical locations where contact with patient is short-term (e.g. examination rooms, recovery rooms, dialysis)
group_1.supply: TN-S preferred. 30mA RCD on socket outlets. Supplementary equipotential bonding within patient environment

group_2.definition: Medical locations where electrical equipment is used in surgical operations, life-supporting treatment, ICU, cardiac procedures
group_2.supply: MEDICAL IT SYSTEM (IT-M) — isolating transformer per room/group with insulation monitoring (IMD), 8-second alarm response time required
group_2.rcd_status: NO RCDs on patient-essential equipment (an RCD trip during surgery is unacceptable)
group_2.supplementary_bonding: Mandatory and extensive — bond all metallic parts within patient environment to local equipotential bonding bar with maximum 0.2Ω to any point
group_2.emergency_lighting: BS 5266-1 plus HTM 06-01 — battery backup minimum 3 hours for operating lights, 24 hours for general escape
group_2.uninterruptible_supply: Required for life-supporting equipment — typically 15 minutes minimum, often 2 hours

additional_references: HTM 06-01 — Electrical services supply and distribution (NHS technical memorandum, takes precedence in NHS sites)
BS EN 60601 series — Medical electrical equipment safety standards (for the equipment, not the installation)
BS EN 61557-8 — Insulation monitoring devices for IT systems

section_711_exhibitions_shows_stands.scope: Temporary electrical installations for exhibitions, shows, and stands.
key_requirements: 30mA RCD on all socket outlets
Each stand limited to 13A circuit unless higher specifically agreed
All cables in armoured or mechanically protected — public traffic
Daily inspection during use (Reg 711.633)
Earthing per host venue — typically TN-S provided by venue

section_712_solar_pv_systems.scope: PV arrays connected to LV installations.
key_requirements: DC isolator at array AND at inverter (Reg 712.537.2.1.1)
DC cables suitable for outdoor + UV exposure (typically tinned copper, double insulated, UV-stable sheath)
Inverter to BS EN 62109-1/2
Type B RCD on AC side if inverter does not have built-in DC fault current detection (Reg 712.411.3.2.1.2)
G99 or G98 application to DNO required for grid-connected (G98 ≤ 16A/phase, G99 above)
Labelling: PV warning labels at array, isolation labels at all isolators, AC labelling at consumer unit identifying solar circuit

section_715_extra_low_voltage_lighting.scope: ELV lighting (typically 12V or 24V) — track-and-strip lighting, garden lights, jewellery display.
key_requirements: Transformer must be short-circuit protected (BS EN 61558-2-6)
Cables sized for full transformer rating, not lamp rating (allow for short circuits)
Open-circuit/no-load operation must be safe (some transformers fail OC)
Maximum cable length per leg to maintain lamp output — typically 5-10m for 12V depending on cable size

section_717_mobile_or_transportable_unit.scope: Mobile catering vans, exhibition trailers, mobile workshops connected via plug to LV supply.
key_requirements: 16A or 32A plug-and-socket to BS EN 60309-2
30mA RCD at the supply or in the unit
Continuity test on plug-cable-socket-unit assembly required before each use
If unit has own generator, follow Section 551 for switching between generator and shore supply (interlocking)

section_722_electric_vehicle_charging.scope: EV charging installations — domestic and commercial.
key_requirements: Open-PEN risk (PME supplies): Reg 722.411.4.1 mandates ONE of: (a) supplied via earth electrode AND PME earth disconnected, (b) open-PEN detection device, (c) supplied through TT island, OR (d) continuous monitoring meeting equivalent safety
RCD: Type A 30mA per charge point minimum (Reg 722.531.3.101); Type B required if charging unit does not have built-in 6mA DC residual detection
Charging unit to BS EN 61851-1
Plug and socket: BS EN 62196 (Type 2 'Mennekes' for AC, CCS Combo 2 for DC)
Cable sizing per BS 7671 — Mode 3 typically 7.4 kW (32A) or 22 kW (32A 3-phase), Mode 4 (DC rapid) 50-350 kW
Dedicated circuit recommended — no shared loads
Labelling: warning label at consumer unit, isolation provisions clearly marked

section_730_onshore_units_shore_connecti._reference: BS 7671:2018 Section 730 (added in AMD 1, expanded in AMD 2)
section_730_onshore_units_shore_connecti.scope: Onshore power supplies for inland navigation vessels and other berthed craft.
key_requirements: TT earthing typical — independent earth at the shore connection
BS EN 60309 high current connectors (typically 63A or 125A 3-phase)
30mA RCD per outlet
Galvanic isolation to prevent stray currents through water

common_oversights: Bathroom 30mA RCD missing (was always required for domestic) — frequent EICR finding
EV charger installed without open-PEN protection on PME supply — major safety issue
Marina pontoon with shared RCD — must be per socket outlet
Construction site with TN-C-S supply — should be TT or TN-S due to PEN break risk
Operating theatre with conventional TN circuits — should be medical IT in Group 2
Solar PV without Type B RCD where required — inverter DC fault undetected
Caravan park socket without dedicated earth electrode — TT requirement
